pr.title: Auto-regenerate the wiki Translation Units page in CI
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Added an `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job to `.github/workflows/build.yml` that regenerates the wiki `Translation-Units.md` page after successful default-branch builds. The job downloads the existing `GALE01_report` artifact, runs `tools/wiki_tu.py`, compares the regenerated wiki page against the current wiki clone, and commits/pushes only when there is a real change. The PR matters because Translation Units wiki data previously drifted unless someone manually ran the generator.

key_files.reason: Adds the new `update-wiki` workflow job that regenerates and pushes the Translation Units wiki page.

what_changed: Added an `update-wiki` job named `Update wiki TU list` to the existing build workflow.
The job is gated to run only on pushes to the repository default branch and only for `doldecomp/melee`, skipping pull requests and forks.
The job depends on `build-ninja`, so it reuses the successful build output instead of triggering a separate build.
The workflow grants `contents: write` and `actions: read` permissions, allowing the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` to download artifacts and push to the repository wiki.
The checkout step uses sparse checkout for `tools` and `reqs`, limiting the workspace to files needed by the wiki generator and its dependencies.
Python 3.x is installed and generator dependencies are installed with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt`.
The job downloads the `GALE01_report` artifact into `report`.
The workflow clones the repository wiki via `https://x-access-token:${GH_TOKEN}@github.com/${repo}.wiki.git`.
The workflow runs `python tools/wiki_tu.py write report/report.json "wiki/Translation-Units.md"` and writes through a temporary `.new` file before replacing the page.
Before committing, the job checks `git diff --quiet -- Translation-Units.md` and exits without pushing if the page is already current.
When changed, the job commits as `github-actions[bot]` with a message including the source commit SHA and pushes to the wiki `master` branch.
A dedicated concurrency group `update-wiki` was added with `cancel-in-progress: false` to avoid racing wiki pushes from multiple master updates.
smart_moves: Reused the existing `GALE01_report` artifact, avoiding extra build time and keeping the wiki update tied to the same build data.
Limited execution to pushes on the default branch of `doldecomp/melee`, which avoids unsafe or noisy wiki writes from PRs and forks.
Used the built-in `${{ github.token }}` with job-level `contents: write`, avoiding a personal access token, machine user, GitHub App, or additional repository secret.
Pushed only when `git diff` detected a page change, preventing empty commits and wiki churn.
Used sparse checkout for `tools` and `reqs` to keep the CI job focused and lightweight.
Placed wiki updates in a dedicated concurrency group so multiple default-branch pushes do not race each other while pushing to the wiki.
Validated the token and wiki-push behavior end-to-end on a fork before merge, according to the PR discussion.
decomp_lessons: Generated project-status documentation should be automated in CI when it is derived from build artifacts; otherwise it will drift from actual decomp progress.
For wiki or report generation jobs, consume existing build artifacts such as `GALE01_report` instead of rebuilding or duplicating the reporting pipeline.
CI jobs that mutate repository-adjacent state, such as a GitHub wiki, should be tightly gated to trusted branches and the canonical repository.
When a generator depends on manually preserved content, the workflow should read or clone the current generated target first; the PR body notes this preserves assignees by reading the current page.
Prefer repository-tracked dependency lists such as `reqs/misc.txt` over hard-coded pip package pins inside workflow YAML.
naming_conventions: `update-wiki` is the workflow job key for wiki regeneration.
`Update wiki TU list` is the human-readable job name.
`Translation-Units.md` is the wiki page filename used by the automation.
`GALE01_report` is the artifact name consumed by the wiki update job.
`GH_TOKEN` is the environment variable used to pass `${{ github.token }}` into the shell script.
`github-actions[bot]` and `41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com` are used for automated wiki commits.
The wiki update commit message format is `Update Translation Units list for ${{ github.sha }}`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The dependency installation should use `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` rather than hard-coded packages; optionally dependencies could be separated further to reduce CI dependencies.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: The final diff installs with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` and sparse-checks out `reqs` as well as `tools`.

review_feedback.author: review discussion
review_feedback.feedback: A reviewer asked whether the wiki push/token setup had been tested on a fork and raised uncertainty around using a non-personal token.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: The author replied that no separate token was needed, validated use of `${{ github.token }}` with `contents: write`, and linked a fork wiki page plus a GitHub Actions run.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Post-review comment: `Nice work on these, thanks.`
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Indicates approval/acceptance, but does not add technical requirements.

searchable_terms: Auto-regenerate the wiki Translation Units page in CI
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs touching `tools/wiki_tu.py` or the Translation Units wiki generator.
Find CI workflow changes that use `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write`.
Find PR #2584 for the related Translation Units generator changes mentioned in this PR body.
Check whether future workflow refactors preserve the `update-wiki` default-branch and canonical-repository guard.
